name: video_kiosk
short_description: Bash automation tool that flashes Raspberry Pi OS and provisions a headless VLC-based video kiosk with Wi-Fi and SSH configuration.
long_description: Video Kiosk is a deployment automation tool for Raspberry Pi systems. It downloads and flashes the official Raspberry Pi OS Lite image to an SD card, configures optional Wi-Fi and SSH access through a .env configuration file, and installs a first-boot systemd service that automatically upgrades the system, installs VLC, provisions SSH users with key-based authentication, hardens SSH configuration, and sets up fullscreen video looping. The script supports both interactive and non-interactive modes, handles both standard SD cards (/dev/sdX) and eMMC devices (/dev/mmcblk0), and provides a complete unattended deployment solution for creating standalone video display kiosks.

modules.description: Main provisioning script that downloads Raspberry Pi OS, flashes to target device, configures Wi-Fi and SSH, and installs the kiosk boot systemd service.
First-boot script that performs system upgrade, installs VLC, configures SSH user with key authentication, hardens sshd, and runs automatic fullscreen video looping on subsequent boots.
Systemd service unit that executes kiosk-boot.sh at every system boot with TTY and journal logging.
Detects boot and root partition identifiers from target block device, handling /dev/sdX and /dev/mmcblk0 naming conventions.
Creates or updates a sudo user with SSH directory, authorized_keys, and proper permissions for key-based authentication.
Applies SSH security hardening by disabling password/root authentication and restricting access to the provisioned sudo user.
Configuration template documenting all .env variables including image URL, mount points, Wi-Fi credentials, SSH settings, and interactive mode options.
